data.title: As a developer, T7 kudos trigger fires for nesting-depth reduction in Python, JS/TS, Ruby, and Java projects
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Replace the Python-specific ast.NodeVisitor implementation of T7 with a lizard-based approach using `lizard -ENS` to measure cognitive complexity (nesting depth) across all supported stacks. Emit TRIGGER_SKIPPED when lizard is not on PATH.

**Why**: T7 today can only measure nesting depth in Python files. Ruby and JavaScript developers who reduce deeply-nested code receive zero kudos. Lizard supports Python, JS/TS, Ruby, and Java, making it the natural polyglot replacement.

**Scope**:
- IN: `lizard -ENS` subprocess with 30s timeout; `--exclude` flag for test directories; compare before/after nesting depth from git diff; TRIGGER_SKIPPED when `lizard` not on PATH; TRIGGER_SKIPPED on non-zero lizard exit; award kudos when nesting depth decreases
- OUT: Installing lizard in CI (covered by SC6 / Story E); T9, T12, T13, T4

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, T7 fires and awards kudos on a JS/TS file where nesting depth decreased (measured by lizard), and does NOT fire on a file where nesting depth increased or stayed flat
  ← Satisfies: "T7 uses lizard -ENS instead of Python ast.NodeVisitor; supports Python, JS/TS, Ruby, Java"
- When this story is complete, T7 emits a machine-readable TRIGGER_SKIPPED signal (not an exception) when `lizard` is not found on PATH
  ← Satisfies: "when lizard absent: TRIGGER_SKIPPED signal"
- When this story is complete, T7 does not hang — subprocess call enforces 30s timeout; timeout → TRIGGER_SKIPPED
  ← Satisfies: "30s timeout"
- When this story is complete, test directories are excluded from lizard analysis (`--exclude` passed for dirs matching test/spec patterns)
  ← Satisfies: "--exclude for test dirs"
- When this story is complete, the TRIGGER_SKIPPED signal fields conform to Kudos Epic 2 (71df-5bd8) schema; no shared library imported from Kudos Epic 2
  ← Satisfies: "All five triggers emit machine-readable TRIGGER_SKIPPED signal"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for: PATH-absent case (TRIGGER_SKIPPED), timeout case (TRIGGER_SKIPPED), nesting-decrease case (award), nesting-flat/increase case (no award)

## Considerations

- [Testing] Mock `subprocess.run` (or equivalent) to avoid requiring lizard binary in test environment; test PATH-absent, timeout, zero-exit-code-with-no-decrease, and award-path cases
- [Maintainability] TRIGGER_SKIPPED signal format: reference Kudos Epic 2 (71df-5bd8) for the required field schema; implement independently (no import from Kudos Epic 2 modules)
- [Reliability] dispatch_kudos_trigger() from Kudos Epic 1 (3e9b-afee) must be mocked in unit tests
- [Reliability] Timeout handling: subprocess timeout=30 must be caught and converted to TRIGGER_SKIPPED, not propagated as an exception

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
